content: Acetylene is a critical feedstock in the chemical industry, widely used for the synthesis of high-value chemicals such as vinyl chloride, acrylonitrile, and various polymers [1].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: However, the presence of ethylene in acetylene streams can lead to unwanted side reactions and reduce the yield of desired products.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Therefore, ensuring the purity of acetylene is essential for maintaining product quality and optimizing the process.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: The separation of C2H2 from C2H4 is particularly challenging due to their similar molecular sizes and physicochemical properties [2].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Furthermore, due to its high reactivity, C2H2 poses significant safety risks, including the potential for explosive mixtures [3].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: As a result, efficient separation technologies are crucial for isolating C2H2 from C2H4, ensuring the safe handling of these gases while enhancing the production efficiency and purity of the final products.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Cryogenic distillation and catalytic hydrogenation are conventional methods used for C2H2/C2H4 separation in the industry [4]; however, they require intensive energy and have high operational costs.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Membrane-based gas separation technology is more advantageous thanks to its energy-saving characteristics, superior separation properties, and small carbon footprint [5–7].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: As in the case of most of the traditional membranes, polymeric membranes are subjected to an inherent trade-off between gas selectivity and permeability, which limits their performance.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Mixed matrix membranes (MMMs) combine the easy processability of polymeric membranes with crystalline materials such as zeolites, metal-organic frameworks (MOFs), and covalent-organic frameworks (COFs), generally offering higher selectivity compared to polymer membranes [8,9].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: This is owing to their well-defined, uniform pore structures and the presence of functional sites, such as open metal centers in MOFs, which enhance specific interactions with target gases, leading to improved selectivity.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: These crystalline fillers contribute to improved separation efficiency owing to their high surface area, well-defined pore structures, and tunable chemical properties, which enable selective adsorption and diffusion of target gases.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Moreover, the compatibility between the filler and polymer matrix plays a critical role in maintaining membrane integrity and stability under operational conditions, thereby optimizing both selectivity and permeability [10–12].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Crystalline materials are explored for their potential in C2H2/C2H4 separation.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: While pristine MOFs and COFs have been extensively studied for this application [13], their utilization as fillers, particularly in the development of MMMs, is gaining attention.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Despite this progress, the membrane separation process of light hydrocarbons is still underdeveloped, and only a few MMMs have been studied to remove acetylene from ethylene [14–16].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: For instance, Xu et al. [14] incorporated three different MOFs (MOF-87, -88, and -89) into 6FDA-ODA, a polyimide-based polymer.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Among the synthesized MMMs, MOF-89/6FDA-ODA with a filler loading of 15 wt.% exhibited the best performance, achieving the permeabilities of 36.2 Barrer for C2H2 and 6.4 Barrer for C2H4, corresponding to a C2H2/C2H4 selectivity of 5.6.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: These values were superior to the neat membrane having C2H2 and C2H4 permeabilities of 8 and 3.4 Barrer, respectively, corresponding to C2H2/C2H4 selectivity of 2.4.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Zhang et al. [15] functionalized a COF with an OH anion, followed by MMM synthesis, employing the same polymer (6FDA-ODA) and tested them for C2H2/C2H4 separation.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: The C2H2 and C2H4 permeability of the MMM having 15 wt.% filler loading was increased to 55.6 and 4.6 Barrer, respectively, and the corresponding C2H2/C2H4 selectivity improved to 12.1.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Similarly, the COF nanosheet was incorporated into 6FDA-ODA, and the resulting MMM with 15 wt.% loading showed improved C2H2 permeability (10 Barrer) and C2H2/C2H4 selectivity (6.7) but reduced C2H4 permeability (1.5 Barrer) compared to the neat membrane [16].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Therefore, the utilization of MMMs for C2H2/C2H4 separation offers great potential, though this area still requires extensive investigation.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Pebax, as a polymer matrix, offers high potential thanks to its unique copolymer structure, comprising polyether (PE) and polyamide (PA) segments, which provide the ability to differentiate between gases based on their polarizability and size [17].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: C2H2 and C2H4 gases present distinct challenges in separation due to their close molecular sizes [18].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: The soft PE segments provide high flexibility and allow for enhanced diffusivity of smaller, linear molecules like C2H2 [19] and offer higher adsorption affinity compared to C2H4 thanks to the presence of C≡C bond.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Moreover, the rigid PA segments contribute to selective gas separation owing to their tight molecular packing and high density [17].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: This structural composition enables Pebax to exploit the size and shape differences between C2H2 and C2H4, facilitating higher permeability for C2H2 and effective separation from C2H4 [20].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Furthermore, the PA segments provide thermal and structural stability, essential for maintaining membrane integrity under operational conditions.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Pebax's flexibility and resistance to plasticization are particularly advantageous in preventing performance degradation due to high-pressure environments and exposure to reactive gases like C2H2, a limitation often observed in more rigid materials such as polyimides [21].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: By leveraging these properties, Pebax can achieve effective C2H2/C2H4 separation, demonstrating its versatility beyond traditional gas pairs.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Additionally, Pebax's compatibility with various fillers, such as zeolites and MOFs, offers opportunities to further enhance membrane performance, optimizing selectivity and permeability for this specific application.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Based on these, we selected Pebax as the polymer matrix in our study, considering (i) the characteristics of the two gases we aim to separate, (ii) compatibility of Pebax with a wide variety of fillers for the synthesis of MMMs, (iii) its structural and thermal stability, and (iv) our previous experience in manufacturing and characterizing MMMs having Pebax as a polymer matrix [22–24].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: As a filler, MIL-100(Fe) (iron(III) trimester) was chosen for its exceptional selective adsorption of C2H2, attributed to the strong interactions between acetylene and the framework's unsaturated Fe3+ sites and surface hydroxyl groups [25].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: These interactions, including π-complexation with the triple bond of C2H2, significantly enhance its binding affinity, enabling precise separation.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Its high surface area, hierarchical pore structure, and commercial availability further support its practicality for gas separation applications.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Besides, the large pores in MIL-100(Fe) provide significant flexibility for post-synthesis modifications, which can be leveraged to further enhance the membrane's performance in subsequent studies.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Based on these reasonings, we incorporated MIL-100(Fe) into the Pebax to synthesize the corresponding MOF/polymer MMMs.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: The combination of crystalline and amorphous structures in Pebax has the potential to offer high gas permeability, mechanical stability, and the robustness needed for effective gas separation, making it a promising polymer for the MMM application [26].

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: Our results demonstrated improved separation performance of the resulting MMMs, showcasing significant advancements in membrane-based C2H2/C2H4 separation.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler

content: This study provides valuable insights and sets a new benchmark for future research in this challenging field.

citation.title: Selective separation of acetylene from ethylene using mixed matrix membranes with MIL-100(Fe) as a filler
